expectations.evidence: Header line 5: 'SENSITIVITY: CONFIDENTIAL -- HR AND HIRING MANAGER USE ONLY'. Also repeated at line 9: '[CONFIDENTIAL -- HR AND HIRING MANAGER USE ONLY]'.

expectations.text: Assesses both candidates across all six dimensions
expectations.evidence: Sarah Chen assessed on: Critical Skills (4 items), Experience (Important requirements section), Performance Trajectory (inferred through platform migration evidence), Readiness Indicators (operating above current level), Development Areas (2 gaps identified with types), Motivation/Career Intent (noted as not yet confirmed). James Okafor assessed on same 6 dimensions. Both receive ratings per dimension.

expectations.text: Classifies Sarah's board presentation gap as an EXPERIENCE GAP with a bridging plan
expectations.evidence: Line 81: 'Gap type: EXPERIENCE GAP. She likely has the communication capability; she needs structured exposure to board-level reporting formats.' Development plan includes: shadow VP in board meetings (Month 1), present one section of board update (Month 2), executive communication coaching (Month 2), present full board update independently (Month 5).

expectations.text: Classifies James's team-scale gap as a meaningful risk factor with a bridging plan
expectations.evidence: Lines 138-143: 'Has not managed teams larger than 15 people...a 4x scale increase...fundamentally different from managing 60+...Gap type: EXPERIENCE GAP (large) — cannot be fully bridged in 6 months without unacceptable risk.' Bridging plan: expand to 25-30 engineers (Month 1), mid-point check (Month 6), expand to 35-40 (Month 6), full reassessment (Month 12).

expectations.text: Provides a specific readiness classification for each candidate
expectations.evidence: Sarah Chen: 'Readiness: READY IN 6 MONTHS' (line 35). James Okafor: 'Readiness: READY IN 12 MONTHS' (line 120). Both use the skill's defined classification system.

expectations.text: Does NOT make a specific promotion commitment
expectations.evidence: Line 285-286: 'NEVER SAY: "You will be the next VP" or "This role is yours." ALWAYS SAY: "If development continues on this trajectory, senior leadership becomes a realistic pathway."' Overall recommendation states 'these assessments inform decisions; they are not decisions' (line 291-292).

expectations.text: Includes retention risk assessment for each candidate
expectations.evidence: Sarah: 'Retention risk: HIGH if not developed' (line 94) with rationale about being 'a prime external hire target'. James: 'Retention risk: MEDIUM if not developed' (line 193) with rationale about timeline and market attractiveness. Comparative table also includes retention risk row.
